key_points: Number systems classify numbers into different categories
Natural numbers start from 1 and continue indefinitely
Whole numbers include natural numbers and zero
Integers include positive numbers, negative numbers and zero
Rational numbers can be written in the form p/q where q ≠ 0
Irrational numbers cannot be written as p/q and have non-terminating non-repeating decimals
Real numbers include both rational and irrational numbers
formulas: Rational number form → p/q where q ≠ 0
exam_tips: Remember that 0 is a whole number but not a natural number
Check if a number can be written as p/q to identify rational numbers
Irrational numbers have non-terminating non-repeating decimals
Square roots of perfect squares are integers
